title: 10 best herbal teas for earache

remedy_properties: Calendic acid: This bioactive compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from earache symptoms.
Carotenoids: The carotenoid content in pot marigold tea has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat infections that may be causing earaches, such as otitis media or externa.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with earache because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the ear. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the earache. This natural remedy may provide quick relief from earache discomfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural alternative to conventional treatments. Its soothing effects can provide much-needed relief.

Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): As an antioxidant, beta-carotene can help protect the ear from oxidative damage, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from earache.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These flavonoids possess anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and combat infection in the ear, providing relief from earache.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are often used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and heal the affected area.
Leaves: They are commonly used in teas for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the ear.
Seeds: They are sometimes used in earache teas for their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers and leaves in quantities of 1-2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Chop the calendula flowers and leaves into smaller pieces to release their active properties and flavors.
Combine the chopped calendula with 8 oz of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before serving and consuming within 24 hours.

Clean and dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and roots for at least 2-3 days.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the echinacea purpurea mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes before straining the liquid with a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the cooled echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day to experience its potential health benefits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals at dawn for optimal potency and store them in a cool dry place.
Combine one teaspoon of dried rosa rugosa petals or two teaspoons of fresh petals with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste, if desired, before drinking slowly while warm.
Repeat the process every 2-3 hours as needed or take two cups with one dose of ear drops for faster relief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hedera helix leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned leaves in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours or until crispy.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried hedera helix leaves and steep them in boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids immediately.
Add honey to taste if desired and drink the warm herbal tea three times daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Harvest cornus florida flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Combine one teaspoon of dried flowers and leaves with one cup boiling water for infusion.

Boil water in a saucepan for three to five minutes and then reduce heat to simmer.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum kok saghyz root and 10-15 grams of dried chamomile flowers.

Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
